---
title: "Simulator"
subtitle: "In-browser Monte Carlo"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-location: right
---

Every slider move triggers a fresh Monte Carlo run. The symbols
used below live on the [Model](model.qmd) page. The desk splits
into zero-capital operating books (fee, b2b, retained, switching)
plus an active treasury at $(k, C_{\mathrm{basis}})$, and the desk
P&L on each sample path is the sample-by-sample sum of `operating`
and `treasury`. The price process is compensated Merton, and
setting $\lambda_J = 0$ recovers pure GBM.

::: {.side-panel-left .side-panel .pc-controls}
### Price process

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof pi0 = Inputs.range([0.1, 10000], { step: 0.01, value: 125, transform: Math.log, label: "π₀ (USD / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof P = Inputs.range([1, 100000], { step: 0.01, value: 1400, transform: Math.log, label: "P (kVCM / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof mu = Inputs.range([-2, 2], { step: 0.005, value: -0.1, label: "drift μ (annualised)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sigma2 = Inputs.range([0, 10], {
  step: 0.001, value: 0.0625, label: "variance σ² (annualised)",
  transform: x => Math.log(x + 1e-3),
  invert: y => Math.max(0, Math.exp(y) - 1e-3),
})
```

### Jumps (Merton)

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof lambdaJ = Inputs.range([0, 100], {
  step: 0.01, value: 20, label: "jump intensity λ_J (/yr)",
  transform: x => Math.log(x + 1e-3),
  invert: y => Math.max(0, Math.exp(y) - 1e-3),
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof muJ = Inputs.range([-0.5, 0.5], { step: 0.01, value: -0.05, label: "mean log-jump μ_J" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sigmaJ = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0.35, label: "log-jump SD σ_J" })
```

### Demand & inventory

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof lambdaDay = Inputs.range([0.001, 10000], { step: 0.001, value: 1, transform: Math.log, label: "retirements (tonnes / day)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof kPre = Inputs.range([0, 1000000], { step: 1000, value: 300000, label: "initial inventory (kVCM)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof cBasis = Inputs.range([0, 100000], { step: 100, value: 20000, label: "inventory basis (USD, total)" })
```

### Contract

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof Tdays = Inputs.range([30, 1095], { step: 5, value: 365, label: "horizon T (days)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof Q = Inputs.range([0.1, 10000], { step: 0.01, value: 175, transform: Math.log, label: "fixed quote Q (USD / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof fee = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0.4, label: "fee rate f" })
```

### Syndication

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof beta = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0, label: "cession β" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof theta = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0, label: "counterparty load θ" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof premiumMode = Inputs.radio(["sharpe", "cvar"], { value: "sharpe", label: "premium mode" })
```

### Switching

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof switchOn = Inputs.toggle({ label: "enable switching", value: false })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof barrierRatioSlider = Inputs.range([0, 10], {
  step: 0.01, value: 1.25, label: "threshold h = H/S₀",
  transform: x => Math.log(x + 1e-3),
  invert: y => Math.max(0, Math.exp(y) - 1e-3),
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Disabled switch ⇒ barrier Infinity.
barrierRatio = switchOn ? barrierRatioSlider : Infinity
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof feePostSlider = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0.4, label: "fee-mode rate f_post" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
feePost = feePostSlider
```

### Monte Carlo

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof nPaths = Inputs.select([2000, 5000, 10000, 20000, 40000], { value: 5000, label: "paths" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof nSteps = Inputs.select([50, 100, 200, 365], { value: 100, label: "steps / path" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof seed = Inputs.range([1, 9999], { step: 1, value: 42, label: "PRNG seed" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
mcResetButton = {
  const defaults = {
    pi0: 125, P: 1400, mu: -0.1, sigma2: 0.0625,
    lambdaJ: 20, muJ: -0.05, sigmaJ: 0.35,
    lambdaDay: 1, kPre: 300000, cBasis: 20000,
    Tdays: 365, Q: 175, fee: 0.4,
    beta: 0, theta: 0, premiumMode: "sharpe",
    switchOn: false, barrierRatioSlider: 1.25,
    feePostSlider: 0.4,
    nPaths: 5000, nSteps: 100, seed: 42,
  };
  const views = {
    pi0: viewof pi0, P: viewof P, mu: viewof mu, sigma2: viewof sigma2,
    lambdaJ: viewof lambdaJ, muJ: viewof muJ, sigmaJ: viewof sigmaJ,
    lambdaDay: viewof lambdaDay, kPre: viewof kPre, cBasis: viewof cBasis,
    Tdays: viewof Tdays, Q: viewof Q, fee: viewof fee,
    beta: viewof beta, theta: viewof theta, premiumMode: viewof premiumMode,
    switchOn: viewof switchOn, barrierRatioSlider: viewof barrierRatioSlider,
    feePostSlider: viewof feePostSlider,
    nPaths: viewof nPaths, nSteps: viewof nSteps, seed: viewof seed,
  };
  const wrap = document.createElement("div");
  wrap.className = "pc-reset";
  const btn = document.createElement("button");
  btn.type = "button";
  btn.textContent = "Reset to defaults";
  btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    for (const k of Object.keys(defaults)) {
      const el = views[k];
      el.value = defaults[k];
      el.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", { bubbles: true }));
    }
  });
  wrap.appendChild(btn);
  return wrap;
}
```

:::

## Derived values

The cards below show the derived quantities that the Monte Carlo
consumes. They are computed from the sidebar inputs and include
$S_0 = \pi_0 / P$, the horizon $T$ in years, the total tonnes
retired $N = \lambda T$, the coverage fraction
$\alpha = \min(1, k / (N P))$, the inventory-exhaustion time
$\tau_{\mathrm{cov}} = \min(T, k / (P \lambda))$ (shown as "inventory
runs out at" in days), and the break-even quote
$Q^* = (1 + f) P S_0 (e^{\mu T} - 1) / (\mu T)$.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
derived = {
  const sigma = Math.sqrt(sigma2);
  const S0 = P > 0 ? pi0 / P : 0;
  const T = Tdays / 365;
  const lambda = lambdaDay * 365;
  const N = lambda * T;
  const tokensNeeded = N * P;
  const coverageDays = (lambda > 0 && P > 0) ? kPre / (lambda * P) * 365 : Infinity;
  const coverageFrac = tokensNeeded > 0 ? Math.min(1, kPre / tokensNeeded) : 0;
  const impliedCost = kPre > 0 ? cBasis / kPre : 0;
  const QStarRatio = expm1OverX(mu * T);
  const QStar = (1 + fee) * P * S0 * QStarRatio;
  return { sigma, S0, T, lambda, N, tokensNeeded, coverageDays, coverageFrac, impliedCost, QStar };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`<div class="derived-stats">
  <div class="card"><div class="label">σ (volatility)</div><div class="value">${derived.sigma.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">S₀ = π₀ / P (USD / kVCM)</div><div class="value">${"$" + derived.S0.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">T (years)</div><div class="value">${derived.T.toFixed(3)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">N = λ·T (tonnes over horizon)</div><div class="value">${derived.N.toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">tokens needed N·P</div><div class="value">${derived.tokensNeeded.toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">coverage from inventory</div><div class="value">${(derived.coverageFrac * 100).toFixed(1)}%</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">inventory runs out at (days)</div><div class="value">${isFinite(derived.coverageDays) ? Math.min(derived.coverageDays, Tdays).toFixed(1) : "∞"}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">implied basis per token</div><div class="value">${kPre > 0 ? "$" + derived.impliedCost.toFixed(3) : "n/a"}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">break-even Q* (USD / tonne)</div><div class="value">${"$" + derived.QStar.toFixed(3)}</div></div>
</div>`
```

The implied basis per token is $C_{\mathrm{basis}} / k$. When it
sits above $S_0$ the inventory carries an unrealised loss; when it
sits below $S_0$ it carries an unrealised gain. Either way the
shape of the custom book's P&L is unchanged and only its location
moves.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
import {
  expm1OverX, xTicksForHorizon, simulate, summarise, simulateSwitching,
  formatTickCurrency,
} from "./lib/compiled/src/core/index.js"
```

## A single run

The summary cards, the table, the histograms, and the sample paths
below refresh on every slider change; a 5,000–10,000-path run
finishes in under a second. Under the [Merton
compensation](model.qmd#jumps) the mean rule on each histogram
holds its position while SD, VaR, and CVaR widen with the jump
sliders. The "premium $\pi_{\mathrm{syn}}$ received" card reports
the up-front syndication premium priced off the b2b book's moments.
At $\theta = 0$ the premium is actuarially fair and equals
$\beta \, \mathbb{E}[\Pi_{\mathrm{b2b}}]$.

The cards below show the top-line numbers of the current run.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
run = {
  const inputs = {
    S0: derived.S0, mu, sigma: derived.sigma, P,
    lambda: derived.lambda, T: derived.T,
    Q, fee, kPre, cBasis,
    beta, premiumLoad: theta, premiumMode,
    lambdaJ, muJ, sigmaJ,
    nPaths, nSteps, seed,
    keepPaths: 20,
  };
  const t0 = performance.now();
  const raw = simulate(inputs);
  const elapsedMs = performance.now() - t0;
  const customDeskSamples = new Float64Array(raw.b2bSamples.length);
  for (let i = 0; i < customDeskSamples.length; i++) {
    customDeskSamples[i] = raw.b2bSamples[i] + raw.treasurySamples[i];
  }
  return {
    inputs,
    ...raw,
    customDeskSamples,
    fee: summarise(raw.feeSamples),
    b2b: summarise(raw.b2bSamples),
    retained: summarise(raw.retainedSamples),
    treasury: summarise(raw.treasurySamples),
    customDesk: summarise(customDeskSamples),
    elapsedMs,
  };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Switching operating book; composed desk deferred to Validation.
switchRun = {
  const inputs = {
    S0: derived.S0, mu, sigma: derived.sigma, P,
    lambda: derived.lambda, T: derived.T,
    Q, fee,
    barrierRatio, feePost,
    lambdaJ, muJ, sigmaJ,
    nPaths, nSteps, seed,
    keepPaths: 0,
  };
  const t0 = performance.now();
  const raw = simulateSwitching(inputs);
  const elapsedMs = performance.now() - t0;
  let everCrossed = 0, tFeeSum = 0, crossingsSum = 0;
  for (let i = 0; i < raw.everCrossedMask.length; i++) {
    everCrossed += raw.everCrossedMask[i];
    tFeeSum += raw.tFeeSamples[i];
    crossingsSum += raw.nCrossingsSamples[i];
  }
  const n = raw.everCrossedMask.length;
  return {
    inputs, ...raw, elapsedMs,
    summary: summarise(raw.pnlSamples),
    probSwitch: everCrossed / n,
    meanTimeInFee: tFeeSum / n,
    meanNCrossings: crossingsSum / n,
  };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`<div class="derived-stats">
  <div class="card"><div class="label">paths simulated</div><div class="value">${nPaths.toLocaleString()}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">wall time</div><div class="value">${run.elapsedMs.toFixed(1)} ms</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">coverage this run</div><div class="value">${(run.tauFrac * 100).toFixed(1)}%</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">leftover tokens at T</div><div class="value">${run.tokensLeftover.toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">cession β</div><div class="value">${(beta * 100).toFixed(0)}%</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">premium π_syn received</div><div class="value">${(run.premium >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(run.premium).toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">custom desk CVaR₉₅</div><div class="value">${(run.customDesk.cvar95 >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(run.customDesk.cvar95).toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">custom desk SD vs. b2b</div><div class="value">${run.b2b.sd > 0 ? (100 * (1 - run.customDesk.sd / run.b2b.sd)).toFixed(1) + "%" : "n/a"}</div></div>
</div>`
```

Enabling switching activates the threshold $H = h S_0$. The cards
below then report the first-passage probability, the mean time in
fee mode, and the mean number of crossings per path.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`${switchOn
  ? `<div class="derived-stats">
    <div class="card"><div class="label">threshold h</div><div class="value">${barrierRatio.toFixed(2)} (H = $${(barrierRatio * derived.S0).toFixed(4)})</div></div>
    <div class="card"><div class="label">f_post</div><div class="value">${feePost.toFixed(3)}</div></div>
    <div class="card"><div class="label">P[ever in fee]</div><div class="value">${(switchRun.probSwitch * 100).toFixed(1)}%</div></div>
    <div class="card"><div class="label">E[T_fee]</div><div class="value">${(switchRun.meanTimeInFee * 365 / derived.T).toFixed(1)} days</div></div>
    <div class="card"><div class="label">E[# crossings]</div><div class="value">${switchRun.meanNCrossings.toFixed(2)}</div></div>
    <div class="card"><div class="label">switching E[Π]</div><div class="value">${(switchRun.summary.mean >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(switchRun.summary.mean).toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
    <div class="card"><div class="label">switching CVaR₉₅</div><div class="value">${(switchRun.summary.cvar95 >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(switchRun.summary.cvar95).toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  </div>`
  : `<div class="derived-stats"><div class="card"><div class="label">switching</div><div class="value">off, toggle "enable switching" in the side panel to activate</div></div></div>`
}`
```

The table below has one row per operating book plus the treasury at
$(k, C_{\mathrm{basis}})$ and the derived custom desk $= b2b +
\text{treasury}$. Each row's $\Pi$ is that book's terminal P&L in
USD over $[0, T]$.

| column | meaning |
| --- | --- |
| $\mathbb{E}[\Pi]$ | sample mean |
| SD | sample SD |
| $\pm \mathrm{CI}_{95}$ | $1.96 \cdot \mathrm{SD} / \sqrt{n}$ |
| $\mathrm{VaR}_{95} / \mathrm{VaR}_{99}$ | 5% / 1% loss thresholds |
| $\mathrm{CVaR}_{95} / \mathrm{CVaR}_{99}$ | tail means |
| $\mathbb{P}[\Pi < 0]$ | loss probability |
| Sharpe | $\mathbb{E}[\Pi] / \mathrm{SD}[\Pi]$ (horizon-absolute) |

P&L is the USD total over $[0, T]$. Divide by $N = \lambda T$ for
a per-tonne figure. As the sliders move, the treasury row opens at
MTM $k \cdot S_0 - C_{\mathrm{basis}}$ and drifts as inventory
burns down, so sweeping $C_{\mathrm{basis}}$ translates its
location without changing its shape. The custom-desk row collapses
to the matched limit at $k = N P$ and $C_{\mathrm{basis}} = N P
S_0$, where $I_T$ cancels between `b2b` and `treasury` consumption
and the desk total becomes the deterministic $N (Q - P S_0)$. At
$k = 0$ the treasury is zero sample by sample and the custom desk
collapses onto `b2b`.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
summaryRows = [
  { book: "fee", ...run.fee },
  { book: "b2b", ...run.b2b },
  { book: "retained (syndicated-on-b2b)", ...run.retained },
  { book: "treasury (k, C_basis)", ...run.treasury },
  { book: "custom desk = b2b + treasury", ...run.customDesk },
  ...(switchOn
    ? [{ book: `switching (h=${barrierRatio.toFixed(2)})`, ...switchRun.summary }]
    : []),
]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Inputs.table(summaryRows, {
  select: false,
  columns: ["book","mean","sd","ci95","var95","var99","cvar95","cvar99","probLoss","sharpe"],
  header: {
    book: "book",
    mean: "E[Π] (mc)",
    sd: "SD",
    ci95: "±CI95",
    var95: "VaR95",
    var99: "VaR99",
    cvar95: "CVaR95",
    cvar99: "CVaR99",
    probLoss: "P[Π<0]",
    sharpe: "Sharpe",
  },
  format: {
    mean: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    sd: x => "$" + x.toFixed(2),
    ci95: x => "±$" + x.toFixed(2),
    var95: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    var99: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    cvar95: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    cvar99: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    probLoss: x => x.toFixed(3),
    sharpe: x => x === null ? "n/a" : (x >= 0 ? "" : "−") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(3),
  }
})
```

### P&L distributions

The panels below show the empirical terminal-P&L density for each
book, one panel per book. The x-axis is terminal P&L in USD and
the y-axis is empirical density. The black rule in each panel is
the Monte Carlo mean, and the dashed grey rule sits at $\Pi = 0$.
The fee panel is non-negative and the b2b panel is its mirror. The
custom-desk panel collapses to a spike at the matched limit.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
includeSwitch = switchOn
switchLabel = includeSwitch ? `switching (h=${barrierRatio.toFixed(2)})` : null
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
pnlRecords = {
  const rows = [];
  const stride = Math.max(1, Math.floor(run.feeSamples.length / 2500));
  for (let i = 0; i < run.feeSamples.length; i += stride) {
    rows.push({ book: "fee", pnl: run.feeSamples[i] });
    rows.push({ book: "b2b", pnl: run.b2bSamples[i] });
    rows.push({ book: "retained (syndicated-on-b2b)", pnl: run.retainedSamples[i] });
    rows.push({ book: "treasury (k, C_basis)", pnl: run.treasurySamples[i] });
    rows.push({ book: "custom desk = b2b + treasury", pnl: run.customDeskSamples[i] });
    if (includeSwitch) rows.push({ book: switchLabel, pnl: switchRun.pnlSamples[i] });
  }
  return rows;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  width: 900,
  height: 720,
  marginLeft: 80,
  marginRight: 30,
  marginBottom: 50,
  grid: true,
  x: { label: "terminal P&L (USD)", tickFormat: formatTickCurrency },
  y: { label: "empirical density" },
  fy: { label: "book" },
  color: { legend: true, domain: [
    "fee","b2b","retained (syndicated-on-b2b)","treasury (k, C_basis)","custom desk = b2b + treasury",
    ...(includeSwitch ? [switchLabel] : []),
  ] },
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleY([0]),
    Plot.ruleX([0], { stroke: "#888", strokeDasharray: "2 4" }),
    Plot.rectY(pnlRecords, Plot.binX({ y: "proportion" }, { x: "pnl", fill: "book", fy: "book", thresholds: 50 })),
    Plot.ruleX([
      { book: "fee", x: run.fee.mean },
      { book: "b2b", x: run.b2b.mean },
      { book: "retained (syndicated-on-b2b)", x: run.retained.mean },
      { book: "treasury (k, C_basis)", x: run.treasury.mean },
      { book: "custom desk = b2b + treasury", x: run.customDesk.mean },
      ...(includeSwitch ? [{ book: switchLabel, x: switchRun.summary.mean }] : []),
    ], { x: "x", fy: "book", stroke: "black", strokeWidth: 2 }),
  ]
})
```

_Black: MC mean. Dashed grey: $\Pi = 0$._

### Price paths

The chart below shows twenty sampled kVCM trajectories as a
function of time, with the first one highlighted in orange and the
others in grey. The green dashed rule marks the
inventory-exhaustion time $\alpha T$. If switching is on, a second
dashed rule marks the fee-mode threshold $H = h S_0$. The
randomness of the custom desk lives on $[\alpha T, T]$, the region
to the right of the green dashed rule.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
pathRecords = {
  const rows = [];
  const paths = run.sampledPaths;
  const nS = run.inputs.nSteps;
  for (let idx = 0; idx < paths.length; idx++) {
    const path = paths[idx];
    const series = idx === 0 ? "first path" : "sampled paths";
    for (let k = 0; k <= nS; k++) {
      rows.push({
        tDays: (k / nS) * Tdays,
        S: path[k],
        id: idx,
        series,
      });
    }
  }
  return rows;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  width: 900,
  height: 340,
  marginLeft: 70,
  marginBottom: 50,
  grid: true,
  x: { label: "t (days)", domain: [0, Tdays], ticks: xTicksForHorizon(Tdays) },
  y: { label: "kVCM spot S_t (USD)", zero: true, tickFormat: formatTickCurrency },
  color: {
    legend: true,
    domain: [
      "sampled paths",
      "first path",
      "αT",
      ...(switchOn ? ["H = h S₀"] : []),
    ],
    range: [
      "#888",
      "#c94",
      "#2a7",
      ...(switchOn ? ["#a36"] : []),
    ],
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleY([0]),
    Plot.line(pathRecords.filter(r => r.id !== 0), { x: "tDays", y: "S", z: "id", stroke: "series", strokeOpacity: 0.35 }),
    Plot.line(pathRecords.filter(r => r.id === 0), { x: "tDays", y: "S", stroke: "series", strokeWidth: 2 }),
    Plot.ruleX([{ t: run.tauFrac * Tdays, kind: "αT" }], { x: "t", stroke: "kind", strokeDasharray: "4 3", strokeWidth: 2 }),
    ...(switchOn
      ? [Plot.ruleY([{ y: barrierRatio * derived.S0, kind: "H = h S₀" }], { y: "y", stroke: "kind", strokeDasharray: "6 3", strokeWidth: 2 })]
      : []),
  ]
})
```
